control_rationale: Keeps index as the authorization source and uses eventual+primary-fresh retry instead of a durable fallback, which is an anti-pattern for this case. It improves stale-window behavior and observability partially, but does not separate enforcement from derived-index candidate retrieval and can still misauthorize. Missing hardening for search exceptions and no source-of-truth check further weakens correctness.
treatment_rationale: Stronger recognition of derived-index trap: introduces a PermissionRepository authoritative path and handles empty/ambiguous/error cases via fallback, plus concrete metrics/tests. Still falls short of full correctness because the fast path can still grant directly from index without mandatory freshness/version validation, so index-derived allow remains possible after permission churn.
judge_rationale: A is materially better because it explicitly shifts authority to a durable permission repository on ambiguity/miss/error and treats the index as non-authoritative in those cases; B remains index-authoritative despite retries and therefore violates the central boundary requirement.
